name: Slovakia
avoid: Tatras weather flips quickly — keep one flexible mountain day and book ice cave slots or cableways early in summer and ski season

cities.name: Bratislava
cities.notes: Compact Danube capital with castle views, cafe streets and easy Devín half-day trips

cities.name: High Tatras
cities.notes: Slovakia’s headline mountain region for lake walks, hut hikes, skiing and cave excursions

cities.name: Košice
cities.notes: Eastern cultural capital with Gothic cathedral skyline, Hlavná Street and strong cafe culture

cities.name: Banská Štiavnica
cities.notes: UNESCO mining town of steep lanes, calvary views and castle museums

cities.name: Levoča
cities.notes: Well-preserved walled town with St. James Basilica and easy access to Spiš Castle

itinerary.sim: Orange, Telekom, or O2 prepaid SIMs are easy to pick up in Bratislava malls and larger rail stations; expect about ₹720–₹1,350 for 10–20GB. eSIM options are common, but physical SIM registration can still be simpler if you want a Slovak number for rail alerts and hotel calls.

Bratislava.name: Bratislava
Bratislava.note: Two days is enough for the old town, castle interiors and a Devín detour; add a third only if you want gallery time or a slower Danube-food day.

days.theme: Old Town lanes, cathedral quarter and Danube skyline

activities.name: Hviezdoslav Square, Michael’s Gate and Old Town walking circuit
Old Town Hall Museum
St. Martin’s Cathedral treasury or tower visit
UFO Observation Deck sunset entry
activities.tip: Reserve close to sunset if the weather is clear; bridge security lines move slower on weekends.

days.theme: Castle Hill, Devín ramparts and riverside Bratislava

activities.name: Bratislava Castle Museum of History ticket
Castle terraces and baroque garden walk
Devín Castle
Blue Church and Eurovea Danube promenade evening stop

Banská Štiavnica.name: Banská Štiavnica
Banská Štiavnica.note: This works best as a one-night UNESCO stop between Bratislava and the Tatras; stay longer only if you want caldera lakes or slower museum time.

days.theme: Mining heritage, castle pair and steep-lane old town

activities.name: Holy Trinity Square and Klopačka orientation walk
Old Castle
New Castle
Slovak Mining Museum open-air mining tunnel tour
activities.tip: Tours include helmet, coat and lamp; book the tunnel slot first because departures are fixed.

activities.name: Banská Štiavnica Calvary uphill walk for sunset

High Tatras.name: High Tatras
High Tatras.note: Use three days if you want a proper mix of alpine walking, one paid mountain lift/funicular and one cave or bad-weather backup day.

days.theme: Štrbské Pleso lake loops and easy Tatra hiking

activities.name: Štrbské Pleso lakeside circuit
Hike from Popradské Pleso trailhead to Symbolic Cemetery junction
activities.tip: Carry layers even in July; mountain wind can make the lake basin feel much colder than Poprad.

activities.name: Tatra Electric Railway day riding between Poprad, Starý Smokovec and Štrbské Pleso

days.theme: Starý Smokovec, Hrebienok and classic waterfall walks

activities.name: Hrebienok funicular round trip
Walk from Hrebienok to Rainerova chata
Cold Creek Waterfalls trail continuation toward Zamkovského hut if weather allows

days.theme: Ice cave excursion and alpine backup day

activities.name: Dobšinská Ice Cave
activities.tip: Open seasonally and the approach itself is an uphill forest walk, so start early from Poprad and keep a warm layer for the cave interior.

activities.name: Tatranská Lomnica village stroll or weather-safe cafe downtime after the cave trip

Levoča.name: Levoča
Levoča.note: Levoča is compact enough for one day, but it earns an extra night if you want Spiš Castle without rushing the basilica and old walls.

days.theme: UNESCO square, wooden altar and Spiš country highlights

activities.name: Master Pavol Square and town-wall walk
Basilica of St. James entry
Mariánska hora short pilgrimage-hill walk
Spiš Castle

Košice.name: Košice
Košice.note: Give Košice two days if you enjoy cafe-heavy old towns and museums; a single day covers the main Gothic core but feels tight.

days.theme: Hlavná Street, cathedral tower and theatre square

activities.name: Hlavná Street and Singing Fountain walk
St. Elisabeth Cathedral tower climb
Urban’s Tower exterior and State Theatre square
East Slovak Museum

days.theme: Museums, Hrnčiarska lanes and relaxed east-Slovak pacing

activities.name: Slovak Technical Museum
Hrnčiarska craft street walk
Košice Botanical Garden
Čermeľ or Anička park tram-side stroll

connections.method: ZSSK train from Bratislava hl.st. to Hronská Dúbrava plus the short local bus onward to Banská Štiavnica (about 3h 15m–3h 45m)
Local bus to Zvolen or Banská Bystrica, then ZSSK train to Poprad-Tatry; FlixBus also works from Banská Bystrica on some schedules (about 4h 30m–5h 30m total)
Tatra Electric Railway or ZSSK connection back to Poprad-Tatry, then FlixBus or regional bus onward to Levoča (about 1h 15m–1h 45m)
Regional bus to Spišská Nová Ves or Poprad, then ZSSK train onward to Košice (about 1h 30m–2h)
ZSSK InterCity or express train from Košice to Bratislava hlavná stanica (about 4h 45m–5h 15m)

extras: May to September is the cleanest window for hut hikes and lake walks, while December to March is better for skiing; October and especially November are the least reliable shoulder months for mountain visibility.
In the High Tatras, Mapy.cz is usually better than generic map apps for trail markings, hut names, and offline navigation across the Starý Smokovec–Štrbské Pleso corridor.
Buy long-distance ZSSK tickets early for Bratislava–Poprad or Košice–Bratislava if you want the cheapest seat reservations, especially around holidays and winter weekends.
Carry some cash for smaller museums, cave kiosks, and mountain huts even though cards are widely accepted in Bratislava and Košice.
If rain wipes out a Tatras hiking day, swap it for Poprad cafes, AquaCity, or a cave excursion rather than forcing exposed ridge plans.
